_meta.note: v3.1 adds context fields to all controls: riskTier, controlObjective, whatGoodLooksLike, keyRiskIfAbsent, samplingGuidance. These feed into the AWP domain worksheets as control context rows. See workprogram/CONTROL-MAPPING.md for full rationale.

domains.name: NCII Governance
domains.description: Governance structure and accountability for NCII.

domains.name: Asset Management
domains.description: Inventory and classification of critical assets.

domains.name: Risk Assessment
domains.description: NCII risk assessment and security level setting.

domains.name: Access Control
domains.description: Identity and access management for critical systems.

domains.name: BCDR
domains.description: Resilience and recovery for critical services.

controls.name: NCII Entity Registration
controls.description: Ensure the organization is properly registered as an NCII entity with NACSA upon designation under Section 17. Maintain up-to-date registration details and designated contact persons.
keyActivities: Respond to NCII designation notice within statutory period (s17)
Appoint and register primary and alternate NCII liaison officers
Maintain register of all designated NCII systems and classification status
Track designation changes and submit updates proactively
controls.controlObjective: Act 854 s17 requires designated NCII entities to register with NACSA and maintain current contact information. This is the foundational obligation — without registration, the entity is invisible to the national cyber security framework and cannot receive directives, threat intelligence, or regulatory guidance.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Designation notice acknowledged within statutory period. Primary and alternate liaison officers registered with NACSA. Internal register of all designated NCII systems maintained and current. Process exists for handling designation scope changes.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Entity operates outside NACSA oversight. Cannot receive threat alerts or CE directives. Non-compliance with s17/s18 carries RM500,000 fine and/or 10 years imprisonment.
controls.samplingGuidance: Full verification — single entity-level control. Verify registration documentation and liaison officer appointments.

controls.name: Governance Structure and Committees
controls.description: Establish board-level governance structure with clear accountability for cyber security. Define steering committee, terms of reference, and reporting cadence. Ensure quarterly board briefings on cyber security posture.
keyActivities: Establish cyber security steering committee with board-level representation
Define and document terms of reference for governance committee
Conduct quarterly board briefings on NCII posture, incidents, and compliance
Maintain governance committee meeting minutes and decision records
controls.controlObjective: Act 854 s18 and s52 require board-level accountability for NCII protection. Governance structures ensure cyber security decisions receive appropriate oversight, resources, and strategic direction. s52 creates personal criminal liability for directors who neglect this duty.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Formally constituted cyber security steering committee with board representation. Documented ToR with defined meeting cadence (quarterly minimum). Minutes showing active engagement — questioning, directing, deciding — not passive receipt of reports. Evidence of risk-informed decision-making.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Directors lack defence against s52 criminal liability (consent/connivance/neglect). Cyber security decisions made without board oversight. No accountability for NCII protection at the highest level.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review last 4 quarters of committee meeting minutes. Full population — governance is entity-level.

controls.name: Leadership Commitment and Budget
controls.description: Demonstrate leadership commitment through signed endorsement of cyber security objectives and dedicated budget allocation for NCII protection.
keyActivities: Obtain signed leadership endorsement of cyber security programme
Allocate dedicated budget for NCII cyber security measures
Review budget adequacy annually against threat landscape
Document leadership decisions on cyber security investments
controls.controlObjective: Leadership commitment translates governance structures into tangible action through resource allocation and visible endorsement. Without dedicated budget and signed commitment, cyber security remains aspirational.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Signed leadership endorsement of cyber security programme. Dedicated, ring-fenced cyber security budget approved by top management. Annual budget review demonstrating adequacy against evolving threat landscape. Budget line items traceable to CoP domain requirements.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Cyber security programme underfunded. Controls implemented ad-hoc without strategic resource allocation. Remediation of audit findings delayed due to budget constraints.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify current FY budget allocation and prior FY review. Single entity-level control.

controls.name: CISO/ICTSO Appointment
controls.description: Appoint a qualified CISO or ICTSO with direct board reporting line. Define RACI matrix for all personnel involved in NCII security.
keyActivities: Appoint CISO/ICTSO with appropriate qualifications
Establish direct reporting line to board for NCII matters
Define and document RACI matrix for NCII security roles
Communicate roles and responsibilities to all relevant personnel
controls.controlObjective: The CoP requires a qualified CISO/ICTSO with direct board reporting to ensure cyber security has authoritative voice at the leadership level. Clear RACI assignments prevent accountability gaps across NCII security responsibilities.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: CISO/ICTSO appointed with documented qualifications (CISSP, CISM, or equivalent). Direct reporting line to board established in org chart. RACI matrix covering all NCII security roles — no gaps in accountability. Roles communicated to all relevant personnel.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: No single point of accountability for NCII cyber security. Reporting lines dilute urgency of security issues. Board uninformed of cyber security posture.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify appointment letter, org chart, and RACI matrix. Single entity-level control.

controls.name: Director Liability Awareness (s52)
controls.description: Ensure directors and officers understand personal criminal liability under s52 of Act 854. Conduct training on consent/connivance/neglect triggers and due diligence defence requirements.
keyActivities: Conduct s52 liability training for all directors and officers
Obtain signed acknowledgement of personal liability obligations
Review D&O insurance coverage for Act 854 criminal proceedings
Assess adequacy of due diligence documentation for each director
controls.controlObjective: s52 creates personal criminal liability for directors through consent, connivance, or neglect. The 'neglect' standard requires NO knowledge or intent — directors must affirmatively demonstrate due diligence. Training and acknowledgement establish the foundation for a defence.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: All directors and officers trained on s52 obligations within onboarding period and annually thereafter. Signed acknowledgements on file. D&O insurance reviewed for Act 854 coverage adequacy (including 10x corporate multiplier). Legal briefing on defence requirements documented.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Directors personally exposed to criminal prosecution without viable defence. D&O insurance may not cover Act 854 proceedings. Board unable to demonstrate 'all due diligence' if challenged.
controls.samplingGuidance: Full population of directors and officers — verify training records and acknowledgements for each individual.

controls.name: Cyber Security Policy
controls.description: Establish and maintain an approved cyber security policy covering all departments, contractors, and third parties. Align with Act 854, CoP, and recognised frameworks.
keyActivities: Develop cyber security policy approved by top management
Ensure policy scope covers all departments, contractors, third parties
Align policy with Act 854, CoP Template, ISO 27001/NIST CSF
Communicate policy and obtain signed acknowledgements (Surat Aku Janji)
controls.controlObjective: s22 requires compliance with the CoP, which mandates an approved, communicated cyber security policy as the foundation for all security activities. The policy defines scope, responsibilities, and alignment with recognised frameworks.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Board-approved policy document current within 12 months. Scope explicitly covers all departments, contractors, and third parties. Alignment mapping to Act 854, CoP Template, and at least one recognised framework (ISO 27001 or NIST CSF). Signed Surat Aku Janji from all personnel with NCII access.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: No authoritative basis for security controls. Personnel unclear on obligations. Non-compliance with CoP 5.0 carries RM200,000 fine and/or 3 years imprisonment under s22.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review current policy version. Sample 10 Surat Aku Janji records across departments per sampling methodology.

controls.name: Policy Review and Amendment
controls.description: Implement annual policy review cycle with version control, formal approval, and event-triggered updates.
keyActivities: Conduct annual policy review with documented outcomes
Maintain version control and approval records
Trigger ad-hoc reviews following incidents or regulatory changes
Track policy amendments with change rationale
controls.controlObjective: Policies must remain current to address evolving threats, regulatory changes, and lessons learned. Annual review ensures the policy reflects the current operating environment and regulatory requirements.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Documented annual review process with version control. Review outcomes recorded with change rationale. Event-triggered reviews conducted after significant incidents or regulatory changes. Prior versions retained for audit trail.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Stale policies that don't reflect current threats or regulatory requirements. Gap between documented policy and actual practice widens over time.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify current version and last 2 prior versions with review records.

controls.name: Cyber Security Objectives
controls.description: Establish SMART cyber security objectives with designated owners, measurable KPIs, and timelines. Track and report progress annually to the board.
keyActivities: Define SMART objectives with owners, metrics, and timelines
Track objective progress through regular measurement
Report annually to board on objective achievement
Update objectives based on threat landscape and audit findings
controls.controlObjective: Measurable objectives translate policy into trackable outcomes. SMART objectives with owners and metrics enable the board to monitor cyber security programme effectiveness and direct resources where needed.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Objectives register with SMART criteria — specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, time-bound. Each objective has a designated owner and defined KPIs. Annual progress report presented to board. Objectives updated based on threat landscape and audit findings.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Cyber security programme operates without measurable targets. Board cannot assess whether security investments are delivering results. No basis for continuous improvement.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review objectives register and last annual progress report. Entity-level control.

controls.name: Cyber Security Resourcing
controls.description: Staff the cyber security function with qualified personnel. Conduct annual resource reviews and allocate adequate budget for tools, training, and audit fees.
keyActivities: Maintain staffing plan for CISO, analysts, engineers, incident responders
Conduct annual resource adequacy review against threat landscape
Allocate and track budget for tools, training, and audit fees
Document job descriptions with required qualifications
controls.controlObjective: NCII protection requires qualified personnel with adequate tools and budget. Annual resource review ensures the security function can keep pace with the threat landscape and regulatory obligations.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Staffing plan with job descriptions specifying required qualifications. Annual resource review documenting adequacy assessment. Budget approved by management covering tools, training, and audit fees. No critical vacancies unfilled for extended periods.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Security function understaffed or under-skilled. Incident response capability degraded. Audit and RA obligations (s21/s23) may not be met due to resource constraints.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review staffing plan, current vacancies, and budget allocation. Entity-level control.

controls.name: Authority and Special Interest Group Contacts
controls.description: Maintain register of authority contacts (NC4, NACSA, RMP, sector lead) with designated liaison officers. Review annually.
keyActivities: Register contacts with NC4, NACSA, RMP, and sector lead
Designate primary and alternate liaison officers
Review authority contact register annually
Test contact channels periodically
controls.controlObjective: s25 requires entities to share threat information with NACSA. Maintaining current contacts with NC4, NACSA, RMP, and sector leads ensures the entity can fulfil notification obligations and receive threat intelligence.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Register of authority contacts with designated liaison officers (primary and alternate). Contact channels tested periodically. Register reviewed and updated annually. Liaison officers aware of their notification obligations.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Entity unable to notify NACSA within 6-hour deadline due to outdated contacts. Threat intelligence from NC4 not received. Non-compliance with s25 carries RM200,000 fine and/or 3 years.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify current contact register. Test one contact channel. Entity-level control.

controls.name: Cyber Security Awareness Programme
controls.description: Deliver cyber security awareness covering phishing, social engineering, incident reporting, and data handling. Require training before NCII system access.
keyActivities: Develop annual awareness programme covering key threat topics
Require training completion before granting NCII system access
Conduct phishing simulations and social engineering exercises
Provide remedial training for assessment failures
controls.controlObjective: Human error remains the primary attack vector. Awareness training reduces the likelihood of successful phishing, social engineering, and insider threats. CoP requires training before NCII system access.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Annual awareness plan covering phishing, social engineering, incident reporting, and data handling. Training completed before NCII system access granted (new employee requirement enforced). Phishing simulations conducted periodically. Remedial training delivered for failures.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Personnel susceptible to phishing and social engineering. Incidents not reported due to lack of awareness. New employees granted NCII access without security orientation.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review training plan and completion rates. Sample 10 new employee records to verify pre-access training.

controls.name: Training Effectiveness Measurement
controls.description: Measure training effectiveness through post-training assessments, phishing simulation results, and feedback. Deliver remedial training where needed.
keyActivities: Conduct post-training quizzes and knowledge assessments
Analyse phishing simulation click rates and report rates
Track training completion percentages by department
Deliver remedial training for personnel who fail assessments
controls.controlObjective: Training delivery without effectiveness measurement is compliance theatre. Measuring outcomes (quiz scores, phishing click rates) ensures the programme actually changes behaviour.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Post-training quiz results with pass/fail thresholds. Phishing simulation results with click rate trends (improving over time). Remedial training records for personnel who failed assessments. Effectiveness metrics reported to management.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Training programme appears compliant but doesn't improve security behaviour. Phishing susceptibility remains unchanged. Resources wasted on ineffective training.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review last 2 phishing simulation results and quiz pass rates. Entity-level metrics.

controls.name: IT Acceptable Use Policy
controls.description: Establish AUP addressing BYOD, remote work, personal use, and violation reporting. Ensure signed acknowledgements and technical enforcement.
keyActivities: Develop AUP covering BYOD, remote work, personal use
Obtain signed Letters of Undertaking from all departments
Implement technical enforcement (web filters, USB restrictions, DLP)
Define violation reporting and consequences
controls.controlObjective: The AUP defines acceptable behaviour for NCII system users, reducing risk from BYOD, personal use, and remote work. Technical enforcement ensures the policy is not merely aspirational.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: AUP covering BYOD, remote work, personal use, and violation consequences. Signed Letters of Undertaking from all departments. Technical enforcement deployed — web filters, USB restrictions, DLP endpoint agents. Violation reporting procedure defined.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Uncontrolled BYOD and personal use introduce malware and data leakage vectors. No basis for disciplinary action on policy violations.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review AUP document. Sample 10 Letters of Undertaking. Verify 3 technical enforcement controls on sample NCII systems.

controls.name: Cyber Security Audit Programme
controls.description: Conduct biennial cyber security audit per s23 by CE-approved auditors. Submit results to CE within 30 days. Track and remediate findings.
keyActivities: Develop audit plan with scope, methodology, and schedule
Engage CE-approved auditors with required qualifications (CISA/CISSP/ISO 27001 LA)
Submit audit report to CE within 30 days via auditpematuhan@nacsa.gov.my
Track remediation of audit findings to closure
controls.controlObjective: s23 mandates biennial cyber security audits by CE-approved auditors with submission to NACSA within 30 days. This is a statutory obligation with criminal penalties — the audit programme is both a control and a legal requirement.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Audit plan documenting scope, methodology, and schedule. Auditors with CISA/CISSP/ISO 27001 LA qualifications and independence declaration. CE submission confirmation within 30-day deadline. Remediation tracker showing finding closure progress.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Non-compliance with s23 carries RM500,000 fine and/or 10 years imprisonment. Audit findings not identified or remediated. NACSA unable to assess entity compliance posture.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify latest audit report, auditor qualifications, and CE submission confirmation. Full verification — statutory obligation.

controls.name: Supplier Relationship Security
controls.description: Manage third-party cyber security risk through vendor assessments, SCM procedures, CSSP licence verification, and contractual cyber security clauses.
keyActivities: Conduct supplier/vendor cyber security risk assessments
Verify CSSP licences per P.U.(A) 221/2024
Include cyber security clauses in vendor contracts
Maintain external service provider register
controls.controlObjective: Third-party vendors with NCII access introduce supply chain risk. P.U.(A) 221/2024 requires CSSP licensing. Vendor contracts must include cyber security obligations to extend the security perimeter.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Vendor register with risk assessments for all critical suppliers. CSSP licences verified against CE register for all cyber security service providers. Contracts include cyber security clauses with right-to-audit provisions. SCM procedures with periodic vendor reassessment.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Unlicensed CSSPs providing services to NCII entity. Supply chain compromise through unassessed vendors. Contractual gaps prevent enforcement of security standards on third parties.
controls.samplingGuidance: Sample 5 vendor assessments per sampling methodology. Verify CSSP licences for all cyber security service providers (full population).

controls.name: NCII Asset Inventory
controls.description: Maintain comprehensive NCII asset inventory covering end-user devices, network equipment, IoT, servers, and software with classification and ownership.
keyActivities: Maintain asset register with ID, location, IP, owner, criticality attributes
Apply classification scheme (Public to Top Secret) to all assets
Assign asset ownership with documented lifecycle tracking
Conduct annual asset inventory review and reconciliation
controls.controlObjective: You cannot protect what you don't know exists. The asset inventory is foundational — it defines the scope of all other controls. s17 designation applies to specific systems, which must be identified, classified, and assigned ownership.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Comprehensive CMDB covering end-user devices, network equipment, IoT, servers, and software. Each asset tagged with classification (Public to Top Secret), owner, location, and criticality. Annual reconciliation between CMDB and network discovery scans. Asset lifecycle tracked from procurement through decommissioning.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: NCII systems unidentified — controls applied inconsistently. Shadow IT on NCII network segments undetected. Vulnerability scanning and patching miss unregistered assets.
controls.samplingGuidance: Run network discovery scan on 2 NCII subnets and compare to CMDB. Sample 10 assets for classification and ownership accuracy.

controls.name: Capacity Management
controls.description: Monitor and manage capacity of NCII systems. Configure alerts for thresholds, conduct annual forecasts, and perform performance testing.
keyActivities: Deploy capacity monitoring for CPU, memory, bandwidth, disk on NCII systems
Configure threshold alerts with defined escalation procedures
Conduct annual capacity forecasts based on growth projections
Perform performance testing for new deployments before go-live
controls.controlObjective: NCII system availability depends on adequate capacity. Monitoring prevents resource exhaustion that could impact essential services. Capacity forecasting supports proactive scaling.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Monitoring tools deployed for CPU, memory, bandwidth, and disk on all NCII systems. Threshold alerts configured with defined escalation. Annual capacity forecasts based on growth projections. Performance testing conducted before new deployments.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: NCII system outages due to resource exhaustion. Capacity issues detected only after impact to essential services. No planning for growth or peak demand.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review 5 capacity threshold alerts from last 90 days. Verify monitoring coverage on sample NCII systems.

controls.name: Risk Assessment Methodology
controls.description: Establish risk assessment methodology aligned to ISO 31000/NIST/PRKM. Define risk criteria including likelihood and impact scales.
keyActivities: Document risk assessment methodology with framework alignment
Define likelihood and impact scales (5x5 matrix or equivalent)
Establish risk appetite and tolerance thresholds
Obtain management approval of methodology
controls.controlObjective: P.U.(A) 219/2024 requires annual risk assessments using an established methodology. A documented, repeatable methodology ensures consistency across assessment cycles and enables meaningful trend analysis.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Documented methodology aligned to ISO 31000, NIST, or PRKM. Likelihood and impact scales defined (5x5 matrix or equivalent). Risk appetite and tolerance thresholds approved by management. Methodology reviewed and updated periodically.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Risk assessments inconsistent across cycles. No common language for risk discussion. Assessment results not comparable year-over-year.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review methodology document. Entity-level control — single verification.

controls.name: Annual Risk Assessment and CE Submission
controls.description: Conduct annual risk assessment per P.U.(A) 219/2024 scoped to NCII systems. Submit results to CE and sector lead within 30 days.
keyActivities: Conduct annual RA by CE-approved persons, scoped to NCII systems
Submit RA results to CE within 30 days via auditpematuhan@nacsa.gov.my
Submit RA results to sector lead within prescribed timeframe
Document submission confirmations and retain for 7 years
controls.controlObjective: s21 mandates annual risk assessment by CE-approved persons with submission to CE within 30 days. This is a statutory obligation — failure carries RM500,000 fine and/or 10 years imprisonment.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: RA report dated within last 12 months. Conducted by CE-approved assessor (approval status verified). Scoped to all NCII systems. CE submission confirmation with timestamp within 30-day deadline. Sector lead submission confirmation if required.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Non-compliance with s21 — criminal penalties. Risk landscape unknown. Controls not aligned to actual threats. NACSA unable to assess national NCII risk posture.
controls.samplingGuidance: Full verification — verify RA report, assessor approval, and submission confirmation. Statutory obligation.

controls.name: Risk Register Maintenance
controls.description: Maintain risk register with identified threats, vulnerabilities, existing controls, and residual risk levels. Prioritise high/critical risks for treatment.
keyActivities: Maintain risk register with threats, vulnerabilities, controls, residual risk
Prioritise risks with high/critical ratings flagged for immediate treatment
Update register continuously as new risks are identified
Present risk register to governance committee quarterly
controls.controlObjective: The risk register operationalises the risk assessment by maintaining a living record of identified risks, their treatments, and residual risk levels. It drives prioritisation of security investments and remediation.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Risk register with identified threats, vulnerabilities, existing controls, and calculated residual risk. High/critical risks flagged for immediate treatment. Register updated continuously as new risks emerge. Presented to governance committee quarterly.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Risks identified in assessment but not tracked to resolution. No mechanism for ongoing risk monitoring between annual assessments. Governance committee unaware of current risk posture.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review risk register currency (last update date). Sample 10 risks for treatment status tracking.

controls.name: Risk Treatment and Monitoring
controls.description: Develop and implement risk treatment plans with assigned owners. Formally accept residual risk. Monitor treatment outcomes continuously.
keyActivities: Develop treatment plan specifying options (mitigate, transfer, avoid, accept)
Assign treatment owners with accountability and timelines
Obtain formal management acceptance of residual risk
Monitor and document post-treatment outcomes
controls.controlObjective: Risk treatment translates assessment findings into action. Treatment plans with assigned owners and timelines ensure risks are actively managed, not just documented. Formal residual risk acceptance creates accountability.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Treatment plan for each risk above tolerance, specifying option (mitigate, transfer, avoid, accept). Each treatment has a named owner with target completion date. Residual risk formally accepted by appropriate management level. Post-treatment outcomes documented and monitored.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Risks identified but not treated. No accountability for risk remediation. Management unaware of residual risk they are implicitly accepting.
controls.samplingGuidance: Sample 10 risk treatment actions per sampling methodology. Verify implementation status against target dates.

controls.name: Change Management Procedure
controls.description: Establish change management procedure covering standard, normal, and emergency changes with impact/risk assessment and CAB governance.
keyActivities: Document change management procedure with change categorisation
Require impact and risk assessment for all change requests
Establish Change Advisory Board with defined membership and authority
Define implementation and rollback procedures
controls.controlObjective: Uncontrolled changes are a primary cause of outages and security incidents. Change management ensures NCII system modifications are assessed for risk, approved by appropriate authority, and documented.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Documented procedure covering standard, normal, and emergency change categories. Change request forms with mandatory impact and risk assessment fields. CAB with defined membership, meeting cadence, and approval authority. Emergency change process with retrospective approval requirement.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Uncontrolled changes introduce vulnerabilities or cause outages. No traceability for system modifications. CAB oversight circumvented.
controls.samplingGuidance: Sample 10 change records per sampling methodology (mix of standard and emergency). Verify approval documentation and PIR completion.

controls.name: Change Implementation and Verification
controls.description: Enforce pre-implementation authorisation for NCII system changes. Conduct post-implementation review (PIR) and apply enhanced scrutiny for changes affecting availability, integrity, or confidentiality.
keyActivities: Verify authorisation timestamps before implementation proceeds
Conduct post-implementation review for all NCII changes
Apply enhanced scrutiny for changes affecting NCII AIC properties
Record and retrospectively approve emergency changes
controls.controlObjective: Pre-implementation authorisation and post-implementation review ensure changes are executed as approved and achieve their intended outcome. Enhanced scrutiny for NCII system changes prevents unauthorised modifications to critical infrastructure.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Authorisation timestamps verified before implementation proceeds. Post-implementation review conducted for all NCII changes. Enhanced scrutiny applied to changes affecting availability, integrity, or confidentiality. Emergency changes documented with retrospective CAB approval.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Changes deployed without proper authorisation. Failed changes not rolled back or reviewed. NCII system integrity compromised by uncontrolled modifications.
controls.samplingGuidance: Sample 5 NCII-impacting changes. Verify authorisation timestamps, PIR records, and rollback plan existence.

controls.name: Unauthorised Change Detection
controls.description: Deploy mechanisms to detect unauthorised changes to NCII systems, including FIM and configuration alerting.
keyActivities: Deploy File Integrity Monitoring on critical NCII system files
Configure configuration change alerts for NCII systems
Investigate and document all unauthorised change detections
Integrate alerts with SIEM for correlation
controls.controlObjective: Detection mechanisms identify changes that bypass the change management process. FIM and configuration alerting provide a safety net for the preventive change control process.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: FIM deployed on critical NCII system files with alerts to SOC. Configuration change alerts integrated with SIEM. All detected unauthorised changes investigated and documented. Root cause addressed and change management process strengthened.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Unauthorised changes undetected — attackers modify system files or configurations without triggering alerts. Insider threats bypass change control process unnoticed.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify FIM deployment on sample NCII systems. Review 5 FIM alerts from last 90 days for investigation records.

controls.name: Data Leakage Prevention (DLP)
controls.description: Deploy endpoint, network, and cloud DLP controls aligned to data classification. Integrate DLP events with SIEM. Ensure s49 secrecy compliance.
keyActivities: Deploy endpoint DLP (USB, email, clipboard, print, screen capture)
Deploy network DLP/inline inspection at egress points
Configure CASB for cloud services with DLP policies
Integrate DLP logs with SIEM for correlation and alerting
controls.controlObjective: s49 imposes strict secrecy obligations on NCII information. DLP controls enforce these obligations technically, preventing data exfiltration through endpoint, network, and cloud channels.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Endpoint DLP agents deployed covering USB, email, clipboard, print, and screen capture. Network DLP inline inspection at all egress points. CASB configured for cloud services. DLP policies aligned to data classification scheme. DLP events integrated with SIEM for correlation.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: NCII data exfiltrated through unmonitored channels. s49 secrecy obligations not technically enforced. Breach of secrecy carries RM500,000 fine and/or 10 years imprisonment.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify DLP deployment on sample NCII systems (3-5 per sampling methodology). Test 3 DLP channels: email, USB, cloud upload.

controls.name: Data Privacy and Protection
controls.description: Establish data privacy procedure covering the full data lifecycle. Align with PDPA Act 709, OSA Act 88, and Act 854 s49. Maintain data processing agreements with third parties.
keyActivities: Document data privacy procedure covering acquisition through disposal
Align with PDPA Act 709, OSA Act 88, and Act 854 s49 secrecy
Execute data processing agreements with all third-party processors
Review data access logs for unauthorised access attempts
controls.controlObjective: NCII entities processing personal data must comply with PDPA Act 709 in addition to Act 854 s49. Data privacy procedures across the full lifecycle — acquisition through disposal — prevent both regulatory breach and reputational harm.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Data privacy procedure covering acquisition, inventory, classification, protection, handling, and disposal. PDPA alignment mapping with identified gaps and remediation. Data processing agreements with all third-party processors. Data access logs reviewed for unauthorised access.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Dual regulatory exposure — PDPA (RM250,000 fine) and Act 854 s49 (RM500,000 fine). Data breach triggers two notification obligations (6-hour NACSA + 72-hour JPDP). Third-party data processing uncontrolled.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review data privacy procedure and PDPA alignment mapping. Sample 5 DPAs with third parties. Review data access logs for last 30 days on 3 NCII systems.

controls.name: Software and Application Security
controls.description: Implement secure coding standards (OWASP) and embed security into the SDLC. Conduct security testing before deployment.
keyActivities: Establish secure coding standards aligned to OWASP Top 10 / ASVS Level 2+
Embed security into SDLC: threat modelling, SAST, DAST, SCA
Conduct security testing (vuln scans, pentesting) before deployment
Maintain qualified testers (OSCP, CREST, or equivalent)
controls.controlObjective: Applications are a primary attack surface. Embedding security into the SDLC catches vulnerabilities before deployment. OWASP alignment ensures coverage of the most common application security risks.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Secure coding standards documented and aligned to OWASP Top 10 / ASVS Level 2+. Security activities embedded at each SDLC phase (requirements, design, implementation, testing, deployment). Security testing (vuln scans, pentesting) conducted before production deployment. Testers hold relevant qualifications.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Vulnerable applications deployed to production. Common application attacks (injection, XSS, IDOR) succeed. Remediation after deployment is 10-30x more expensive than prevention.
controls.samplingGuidance: Sample 3 recent application deployments. Verify security testing was conducted before go-live with results documented.

controls.name: Secure Configuration and Hardening
controls.description: Establish secure configuration baselines aligned to CIS Benchmarks or DISA STIGs. Deploy FIM and configuration drift detection on NCII systems.
keyActivities: Define baseline configurations per OS/platform aligned to CIS/DISA STIGs
Deploy File Integrity Monitoring on NCII system files
Implement configuration drift detection and remediation
Maintain configuration logs with who/what/when/why and integrity protection
controls.controlObjective: Default configurations are insecure. Hardening baselines aligned to CIS Benchmarks or DISA STIGs reduce the attack surface. FIM and drift detection ensure configurations remain hardened over time.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Baseline configurations defined per OS/platform aligned to CIS Benchmarks or DISA STIGs. FIM deployed on critical NCII system files. Configuration drift detection runs periodically with deviations investigated and remediated. Configuration change logs maintained with integrity protection.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Systems running default configurations with unnecessary services and weak settings. Configuration drift goes undetected — hardened systems regress over time. No audit trail for configuration changes.
controls.samplingGuidance: Run CIS benchmark scan on 3 sample NCII systems. Verify FIM deployment and review 5 drift detection alerts.

controls.name: NDA Framework and Templates
controls.description: Establish NDA templates with four required elements: confidential information definition, receiving party obligations, permitted use, and breach consequences. Explicitly reference NCII designation and s49.
keyActivities: Develop NDA templates with 4 required elements
Include explicit reference to NCII designation and s49 secrecy obligations
Obtain legal counsel review and approval of NDA templates
Maintain NDA templates for different relationship types (employee, contractor, vendor)
controls.controlObjective: NDAs are the contractual mechanism for enforcing s49 secrecy obligations. Legally enforceable NDAs with explicit NCII references create a contractual defence and enable legal action against breaches.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: NDA templates containing all 4 required elements: confidential information definition, receiving party obligations, permitted use, and breach consequences. Explicit reference to NCII designation and s49 obligations. Legal counsel review and approval documented. Templates maintained for different relationship types.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: NDAs unenforceable due to missing elements. s49 secrecy obligations not contractually extended to third parties. No legal remedy for confidentiality breaches.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review NDA templates (all types). Verify legal counsel approval. Entity-level control.

controls.name: NDA Execution and Compliance Monitoring
controls.description: Ensure NDAs are signed by all employees, contractors, and vendors with NCII access. Monitor compliance and conduct periodic re-confirmation.
keyActivities: Obtain signed NDAs from all employees, contractors, and vendors
Track NDA coverage and identify gaps
Conduct periodic NDA compliance audits
Re-confirm NDAs at contract renewal or role change
controls.controlObjective: NDA templates are only effective when executed. Full coverage across employees, contractors, and vendors ensures all parties with NCII access are contractually bound. Periodic re-confirmation catches role changes and contract renewals.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Signed NDAs on file for all employees, contractors, and vendors with NCII access. Coverage tracking identifies gaps. Compliance audits conducted periodically. NDAs re-confirmed at contract renewal or role change.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Personnel with NCII access not contractually bound to secrecy. Vendor staff access NCII systems without NDA coverage. Legal basis for enforcement absent.
controls.samplingGuidance: Sample 10 signed NDAs across employees (5), contractors (3), vendors (2) per sampling methodology.

controls.name: Physical Security Procedure
controls.description: Establish physical security procedure addressing access control, CCTV, visitor management, and emergency response. Conduct annual reviews and physical risk assessments.
keyActivities: Document physical security procedure aligned to ISO 27001
Define security zones: outer, restricted, high-security
Conduct physical security risk assessment
Review procedure annually
controls.controlObjective: Physical access to NCII systems is a direct threat vector. Security zones, access controls, and emergency procedures protect against unauthorised physical access, theft, and environmental threats.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Documented physical security procedure aligned to ISO 27001 physical controls. Security zones defined (outer, restricted, high-security) with appropriate controls per zone. Physical security risk assessment conducted. Procedure reviewed annually.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Unauthorised physical access to NCII systems. No security zone differentiation — all areas treated equally. Environmental threats (fire, flood) not addressed.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review procedure document and zone classification. Conduct physical inspection of 2 NCII facilities.

controls.name: Physical Access and CCTV Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor and review physical access logs. Maintain CCTV coverage with 30-day retention. Conduct security patrols.
keyActivities: Review access logs documenting who, when, where
Maintain CCTV coverage map with 30-day footage retention
Conduct and document security patrols
Maintain fire detection and suppression records
controls.controlObjective: Access logs and CCTV provide both deterrent and forensic capability. 30-day CCTV retention ensures evidence is available for incident investigation. Security patrols provide human verification of physical controls.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Access logs reviewed regularly documenting who, when, where. CCTV coverage map showing all entry points and critical areas. 30-day footage retention verified. Security patrol logs maintained. Fire detection and suppression systems operational.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Physical security breaches undetected. CCTV footage unavailable for investigation due to insufficient retention. Access patterns not monitored for anomalies.
controls.samplingGuidance: Request CCTV playback for 3 random dates/times. Review access logs for 5 random dates. Verify patrol log entries.

controls.name: Visitor Management
controls.description: Control and log visitor access to NCII premises. Maintain visitor management procedure with sign-in/sign-out tracking.
keyActivities: Implement visitor management procedure with escort requirements
Maintain visitor logs with sign-in and sign-out times
Issue and retrieve visitor badges
Restrict visitor access to authorised areas only
controls.controlObjective: Visitors represent an unvetted presence in controlled areas. Visitor management controls prevent unauthorised access, ensure escorts, and maintain an audit trail of all non-staff presence.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Visitor management procedure with escort requirements for restricted areas. Visitor logs with sign-in/sign-out times. Visitor badges issued and retrieved. Access restricted to authorised areas only.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Visitors wander unescorted in NCII areas. No record of who was present during a security incident. Visitor badges retained and reused for unauthorised access.
controls.samplingGuidance: Compare 10 visitor badge records to visitor log entries. Verify escort procedures through observation.

controls.name: Network Architecture and Segmentation
controls.description: Establish network security policy with documented architecture diagrams showing segmentation, DMZ, and NCII zones. Review annually.
keyActivities: Document network security policy
Maintain physical and logical network architecture diagrams
Implement network segmentation with NCII zones and DMZ
Review network security policy annually
controls.controlObjective: Network segmentation is a foundational defence — it limits lateral movement and contains breaches. Architecture diagrams provide the baseline for all network security testing and incident response.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Network security policy approved and current. Physical and logical architecture diagrams showing segmentation, DMZ, and NCII zones. Diagrams maintained continuously — updated within 30 days of infrastructure changes. Annual policy review completed.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Flat network allows lateral movement from compromised system to all NCII assets. Architecture unknown — incident responders cannot contain breaches. Network security controls deployed inconsistently.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review network diagrams and verify accuracy against 3 sample network segments through scan or inspection.

controls.name: Network Security Controls (Firewall/IDS/IPS/NAC)
controls.description: Deploy and maintain firewall, IDS/IPS, and NAC controls. Document and review rule sets.
keyActivities: Document and periodically review firewall rule sets
Configure and tune IDS/IPS with monitoring procedures
Enforce network access control (NAC) for device compliance
Maintain rule change records with approval documentation
controls.controlObjective: Firewall, IDS/IPS, and NAC are the primary network boundary and segmentation enforcement controls. Documented rule sets ensure these controls are intentional, reviewed, and aligned to the architecture.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Firewall rules documented with business justification. No ANY-ANY rules on NCII segments. IDS/IPS configured with current signatures and active monitoring. NAC enforcing device compliance before network access. Rule changes approved through change management.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Overly permissive firewall rules expose NCII systems. IDS/IPS with outdated signatures miss current threats. Non-compliant devices access NCII network unchallenged.
controls.samplingGuidance: Export firewall ruleset — analyse for ANY-ANY rules and zero-hit-count rules. Test IDS/IPS with 5 known signatures. Attempt NAC bypass with non-compliant device.

controls.name: System Hardening
controls.description: Apply system hardening standards aligned to CIS Benchmarks or DISA STIGs. Conduct verification scans and remediate deviations.
keyActivities: Define hardening standards per OS/platform aligned to CIS/DISA STIGs
Apply baseline configurations to all NCII systems
Conduct hardening verification scans periodically
Remediate deviations and document exceptions
controls.controlObjective: Default OS and application configurations contain unnecessary services and weak settings. CIS/DISA-aligned hardening reduces the attack surface to the minimum required for business function.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Hardening standards defined per OS/platform with CIS Benchmark or DISA STIG mapping. Baseline configurations applied to all NCII systems. Verification scans conducted periodically. Deviations investigated and remediated or documented as exceptions.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Unnecessary services running on NCII systems provide additional attack surface. Default credentials and configurations exploitable. No baseline to detect drift.
controls.samplingGuidance: Run CIS benchmark scan on 3 sample NCII systems (different OS/platforms). Review deviation remediation records.

controls.name: Endpoint Protection (EDR/AV)
controls.description: Deploy endpoint protection (EDR/AV) across all NCII endpoints with policy configuration and malware detection logging.
keyActivities: Deploy EDR/AV on all NCII endpoints
Configure endpoint protection policies
Monitor malware detection and response logs
Maintain endpoint compliance reports
controls.controlObjective: Endpoints are the most common initial access point for attackers. EDR/AV deployment across all NCII endpoints provides detection and response capability at the point of compromise.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: EDR/AV deployed on all NCII endpoints with current definitions/signatures. Policy configured for real-time scanning, auto-remediation, and alert forwarding to SOC. Malware detection and response logs maintained. Endpoint compliance reports showing coverage and health status.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Malware infections undetected on NCII endpoints. No visibility into endpoint-level compromise. Ransomware or wiper malware propagates unchecked.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify EDR/AV deployment on sample NCII endpoints (per sampling methodology). Review 5 malware detection events from last 90 days.

controls.name: Secure Communications
controls.description: Implement email security (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), TLS configuration, and wireless security standards.
keyActivities: Configure email security: SPF, DKIM, DMARC
Enforce TLS for web and API communications
Implement wireless security standards (WPA3/WPA2-Enterprise)
Maintain secure file transfer mechanisms
controls.controlObjective: Data in transit between NCII systems must be protected from interception. Email security prevents spoofing and phishing. TLS and wireless standards ensure confidentiality across communication channels.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Email security configured: SPF, DKIM, DMARC with enforcement policy. TLS 1.2+ enforced for all web and API communications. Wireless networks using WPA3 or WPA2-Enterprise. Secure file transfer mechanisms for sensitive data.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Email spoofing enables phishing campaigns. Data intercepted in transit on unencrypted channels. Wireless networks vulnerable to eavesdropping.
controls.samplingGuidance: Check SPF, DKIM, DMARC configuration. Test TLS version on 3 NCII web applications. Verify wireless security standard.

controls.name: Identity and Access Management Policy
controls.description: Establish IAM policy covering all account types. Implement account provisioning/deprovisioning with approvals and timely revocation.
keyActivities: Document IAM policy covering admin, standard, vendor, service accounts
Implement provisioning/deprovisioning with formal approval workflow
Enforce timely access revocation on termination, role change, contract expiry
Conduct periodic user access reviews (quarterly minimum)
controls.controlObjective: Access control is fundamental to NCII protection. IAM policy ensures only authorised personnel access NCII systems with appropriate privileges. Timely revocation prevents former employees and contractors from retaining access.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: IAM policy covering all account types. Provisioning requires formal approval with business justification. Access reviews conducted quarterly. Revocation within policy SLA (e.g., 24 hours for termination). Deprovisioning triggered by HR termination process.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Unauthorised access to NCII systems. Former employees retain active credentials. Excessive privileges accumulate over time without review.
controls.samplingGuidance: Sample 5 recently departed staff — verify access revoked within SLA. Review latest quarterly access review. Sample 10 provisioning records for approval documentation.

controls.name: Multi-Factor Authentication
controls.description: Enforce MFA for external, remote, and privileged access to NCII systems.
keyActivities: Deploy MFA for all external and remote access to NCII systems
Enforce MFA for privileged account access
Track MFA coverage percentage across NCII systems
Test MFA enforcement by attempting login without MFA completion
controls.controlObjective: Passwords alone are insufficient — credential theft is the most common initial access technique. MFA provides defence-in-depth for authentication, particularly for external, remote, and privileged access to NCII systems.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: MFA enforced for all external and remote access to NCII systems. MFA enforced for all privileged account access. MFA coverage report showing percentage of NCII systems with MFA enabled (target: 100%). MFA bypass attempts logged and investigated.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Stolen credentials provide direct access to NCII systems. Remote access vulnerable to credential stuffing and phishing. Privileged account compromise without MFA has maximum blast radius.
controls.samplingGuidance: Attempt login to 3 NCII systems without completing MFA — verify all reject. Check MFA coverage percentage report.

controls.name: Privileged Access Management (PAM)
controls.description: Maintain privileged account inventory. Deploy PAM solution with session monitoring, recording, and quarterly access reviews.
keyActivities: Maintain complete privileged account inventory
Deploy PAM solution for session monitoring and recording
Conduct quarterly privileged access reviews
Implement emergency/break-glass procedures with post-use review
controls.controlObjective: Privileged accounts have the highest blast radius — a compromised admin account can access any system, modify any configuration, and exfiltrate any data. PAM controls limit, monitor, and audit privileged access.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Complete privileged account inventory. PAM solution deployed with session monitoring and recording. Quarterly privileged access reviews with justification verification. Emergency/break-glass procedures with post-use review and audit trail.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Unknown privileged accounts exist. Privileged sessions unmonitored — insider threats undetected. Break-glass accounts used without oversight.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review privileged account inventory for completeness. Sample 10 admin accounts — verify current business justification. Review 3 PAM session recordings.

controls.name: Segregation of Duties and Authentication
controls.description: Implement SoD matrix with conflict detection. Enforce authentication policy including complexity, rotation, and lockout.
keyActivities: Define and maintain SoD matrix for critical functions
Detect and resolve SoD conflicts or document compensating controls
Enforce authentication policy (complexity, rotation, lockout)
Review SoD conflicts periodically
controls.controlObjective: SoD prevents any single individual from having control over all aspects of a critical transaction or process. Combined with strong authentication policy, SoD reduces fraud and error risk on NCII systems.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: SoD matrix defining incompatible roles for critical functions. Automated conflict detection in IAM system. Unresolved conflicts have documented compensating controls. Authentication policy enforcing complexity, rotation, and lockout.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Single individual can initiate and approve critical changes. No detection of conflicting role assignments. Weak passwords on NCII systems.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review 10 user accounts against SoD matrix for conflicts. Verify authentication policy technical enforcement.

controls.name: Vulnerability Management Programme
controls.description: Establish VMP with scope, prioritisation, severity-to-SLA mapping, and CVE feed subscriptions. Align with NIST SP 800-40.
keyActivities: Document VMP covering scope, prioritisation, SLAs, exception process
Define severity-to-SLA mapping (Critical 7d, High 30d, Medium 90d, Low next maint.)
Subscribe to CVE feeds: NVD, NC4/MyCERT, CISA KEV, vendor advisories
Triage advisories within 24 hours against asset inventory
controls.controlObjective: Vulnerabilities are the entry point for exploitation. A structured VMP with severity-based SLAs ensures vulnerabilities are identified, prioritised, and remediated before they can be exploited against NCII systems.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Documented VMP covering scope, prioritisation methodology, and exception process. Severity-to-SLA mapping: Critical 7 days, High 30 days, Medium 90 days, Low next maintenance. CVE feed subscriptions active (NVD, NC4/MyCERT, CISA KEV). Advisories triaged within 24 hours against asset inventory.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Vulnerabilities discovered but not remediated within acceptable timeframes. Known exploited vulnerabilities remain unpatched. No prioritisation — critical and low vulnerabilities treated equally.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review VMP document. Verify CVE feed subscriptions. Sample 10 critical vulnerabilities — verify remediation within 7-day SLA.

controls.name: Vulnerability Scanning
controls.description: Conduct automated vulnerability scanning (authenticated and unauthenticated) with target 100% NCII asset coverage.
keyActivities: Deploy automated scanning tools with current licences and feeds
Conduct authenticated (credentialed) scans for internal NCII assets
Conduct both authenticated and unauthenticated scans for external assets
Track and report scan coverage percentage (target: 100%)
controls.controlObjective: Automated scanning provides systematic discovery of vulnerabilities across the NCII asset inventory. Authenticated scans detect configuration issues invisible to unauthenticated scans. 100% coverage ensures no blind spots.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Scanning tools deployed with current licences and vulnerability feeds. Authenticated scans for internal NCII assets. Both authenticated and unauthenticated scans for external assets. Scan coverage = 100% of NCII assets within defined frequency.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Vulnerabilities on NCII systems undetected. Unauthenticated-only scans miss 40-60% of vulnerabilities. Coverage gaps leave entire system populations unscanned.
controls.samplingGuidance: Compare scan scope (IP ranges) to CMDB asset inventory. Verify scan coverage percentage. Review scan configuration for authenticated access.

controls.name: Penetration Testing
controls.description: Conduct manual penetration testing by qualified professionals (OSCP, CEH, GPEN, CREST). Retest after remediation.
keyActivities: Engage qualified penetration testers (OSCP, CEH, GPEN, CREST)
Conduct external, internal, and application-layer penetration tests
Retest after remediation to confirm vulnerability resolution
Cross-reference pentest findings with scan results
controls.controlObjective: Automated scanning finds known vulnerabilities; penetration testing finds exploitable attack paths. Qualified testers simulate real-world attacks to validate whether controls actually prevent compromise.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Penetration tests conducted by qualified professionals (OSCP, CEH, GPEN, CREST). Scope covers external, internal, and application layers. Findings remediated and retested to confirm resolution. Cross-reference between pentest findings and scan results.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Exploitable attack paths unidentified. Vulnerability scan results not validated by real-world testing. Remediation effectiveness unverified.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review latest pentest report. Verify tester qualifications. Sample 5 pentest findings — verify remediation and retest.

controls.name: Vulnerability Remediation and Threat Intelligence
controls.description: Track vulnerability remediation against SLAs. Integrate threat intelligence for reprioritisation based on active exploitation.
keyActivities: Maintain vulnerability remediation tracker with SLA compliance metrics
Integrate threat intelligence (CISA KEV, NC4) for reprioritisation
Escalate SLA breaches to management
Report remediation metrics to governance committee
controls.controlObjective: Identifying vulnerabilities without tracking remediation to completion creates a false sense of security. SLA compliance metrics and threat intelligence integration ensure the most dangerous vulnerabilities are fixed first.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Vulnerability remediation tracker with SLA compliance metrics per severity. Threat intelligence (CISA KEV, NC4 advisories) integrated for reprioritisation based on active exploitation. SLA breaches escalated to management. Remediation metrics reported to governance committee.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Vulnerabilities identified but remediation stalled. Known exploited vulnerabilities not prioritised over theoretical risks. No accountability for SLA breaches.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review remediation tracker. Verify SLA compliance rates by severity. Sample 5 actively exploited vulnerabilities (CISA KEV) — verify prioritised remediation.

controls.name: Security Monitoring Procedure (SMP)
controls.description: Establish Security Monitoring Procedure defining objectives, scope, tools, roles, and review cadence. Maintain SOC team roster and on-call rotations.
keyActivities: Document SMP with objectives, scope, tools, and roles
Maintain SOC team roster and on-call rotation schedule
Conduct annual SMP review
Trigger ad-hoc reviews following infrastructure changes
controls.controlObjective: The SMP defines what is monitored, how, by whom, and to what standard. Without a documented procedure, monitoring is ad-hoc and inconsistent. SOC team readiness ensures 24/7 detection capability.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Documented SMP with clear objectives, defined scope (all NCII systems), tool inventory, and role assignments. SOC team roster with on-call rotations ensuring 24/7 coverage. SMP reviewed annually and after infrastructure changes.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Monitoring gaps — some NCII systems not covered. SOC understaffed or no after-hours coverage. No defined process for handling detected events.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review SMP document. Verify SOC roster and on-call schedule. Entity-level control.

controls.name: SIEM and Log Source Management
controls.description: Ensure SIEM log source completeness across all NCII systems. Track coverage percentage and maintain correlation rules mapped to MITRE ATT&CK.
keyActivities: Verify SIEM log sources: servers, network, security controls, apps, identity, cloud
Track log source coverage percentage (target: 100%)
Develop and maintain correlation rules mapped to MITRE ATT&CK tactics
Conduct periodic log source gap analysis
controls.controlObjective: SIEM log completeness determines detection capability. If a log source is missing, attacks via that vector are invisible. Correlation rules mapped to MITRE ATT&CK ensure detection covers known adversary techniques.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: SIEM log sources cover: servers, network devices, security controls, applications, identity systems, and cloud infrastructure. Log source coverage = 100% of NCII assets. Correlation rules mapped to MITRE ATT&CK tactics with periodic gap analysis. Log retention meets regulatory requirements.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Blind spots in detection — attacks via uncovered log sources invisible. Correlation rules miss common attack techniques. Insufficient log retention prevents forensic investigation.
controls.samplingGuidance: Cross-reference SIEM log source inventory against CMDB. Calculate coverage percentage. Sample-review 10 correlation rules for quality and MITRE mapping.

controls.name: Alert Triage and SOC Operations
controls.description: Enforce alert triage SLAs, manage false positive rates through tuning, and measure MTTD/MTTR KPIs.
keyActivities: Enforce alert triage SLAs (Critical 15min, High 1hr, Medium 4hr, Low 24hr)
Analyse and manage false positive rate (< 70% threshold)
Measure and report MTTD and MTTR KPIs
Tune correlation rules based on false positive analysis
controls.controlObjective: Detection without timely triage is detection without response. Alert SLAs ensure critical events are investigated within minutes, not hours. False positive management prevents alert fatigue that causes real threats to be missed.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Alert triage SLAs enforced: Critical 15 min, High 1 hour, Medium 4 hours, Low 24 hours. False positive rate < 70% (higher indicates tuning problem). MTTD and MTTR KPIs measured and trending downward. Triage decisions recorded for audit trail.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Critical alerts sit uninvestigated for hours. Alert fatigue from high false positive rates causes SOC analysts to ignore real threats. No KPIs to measure or improve detection performance.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review 20 alerts from last 30 days — verify triage within SLA. Calculate false positive rate. Verify MTTD/MTTR raw data.

controls.name: Threat Intelligence Integration
controls.description: Integrate threat intelligence feeds (STIX/TAXII, NC4, commercial) with automatic IoC correlation in SIEM.
keyActivities: Subscribe to threat intelligence feeds (STIX/TAXII, NC4, commercial)
Configure automatic IoC correlation in SIEM
Share relevant threat information with CE per s25
Track feed freshness and coverage
controls.controlObjective: Threat intelligence transforms reactive detection into proactive defence. Automated IoC correlation in SIEM enables detection of known threat actor infrastructure. s25 requires sharing relevant threat information with NACSA.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Threat intelligence feeds subscribed: STIX/TAXII, NC4, and at least one commercial feed. Automatic IoC correlation configured in SIEM. Feed freshness tracked — updates within 24 hours of publication. Relevant threat information shared with CE per s25.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Known malicious infrastructure not detected in network traffic. Threats identified by others not incorporated into detection. s25 threat sharing obligation not met.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify feed subscriptions and update timestamps. Test IoC correlation with known test indicators.

controls.name: Incident Management Plan (CSIMP)
controls.description: Establish CSIMP covering full lifecycle (Plan/Detect/Respond/Update). Define incident classification, escalation procedures, and review cadence.
keyActivities: Document CSIMP covering Plan/Detect/Respond/Update lifecycle
Define incident classification scheme and severity levels
Establish escalation procedures with RACI matrix
Review CSIMP annually and after significant incidents
controls.controlObjective: The CSIMP provides the playbook for incident response — without it, response is ad-hoc and slow. Post-incident reviews and annual updates ensure the plan incorporates lessons learned.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: CSIMP documented covering Plan/Detect/Respond/Update lifecycle. Incident classification scheme with severity levels defined. Escalation procedures with RACI matrix. Post-incident review conducted after every significant incident. CSIMP reviewed annually and after incidents.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Chaotic incident response — no defined roles, escalation paths, or procedures. Lessons not learned — same failures recur. Response time exceeds 6-hour notification deadline.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review CSIMP document. Verify last post-incident review. Confirm annual review completed. Entity-level control.

controls.name: CSIRT Establishment
controls.description: Establish CSIRT with defined membership, roles, and authority. Designate 3 authorised persons per s11(3) with 24/7 contactability.
keyActivities: Establish CSIRT with documented charter, membership, and authority
Designate 3 authorised persons per s11(3) for NACSA notification
Register authorised persons with NACSA
Ensure 24/7 contactability for all authorised persons
controls.controlObjective: s11(3) requires 3 authorised persons for NACSA notification. The CSIRT provides the organisational capability for incident detection, response, and coordination. 24/7 contactability ensures incidents at any hour can be handled.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: CSIRT established with documented charter, membership, roles, and authority. 3 authorised persons designated per s11(3) and registered with NACSA. 24/7 contactability arrangements documented and tested. CSIRT members trained and exercised.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: No organisational capability for coordinated incident response. Authorised persons unreachable during after-hours incident. NACSA notification fails due to unregistered personnel.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify CSIRT charter and membership. Confirm 3 authorised person appointment letters. Test after-hours contactability.

controls.name: 6-Hour Incident Notification Process
controls.description: Implement 6-hour notification process: immediate alert to cert@nc4.gov.my, formal report via NC4 portal, and 14-day supplementary submission. Validate through tabletop exercises.
keyActivities: Document immediate notification to cert@nc4.gov.my
Establish formal report submission via NC4 portal within 6 hours
Implement 14-day supplementary information submission with root cause analysis
Conduct 6-hour notification tabletop walkthrough exercise
controls.controlObjective: s26 mandates notification within 6 hours of a significant-impact incident — this is the tightest operational deadline in Act 854. Failure carries RM500,000 fine plus RM5,000/day continuing penalty. The notification process must be rehearsed and validated.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Immediate notification workflow to cert@nc4.gov.my documented and tested. Formal report submission via NC4 portal (www.nc4.gov.my) within 6 hours. 14-day supplementary submission process with calendar reminders. 6-hour tabletop walkthrough conducted with timeline evidence. NC4 portal access verified for 2+ authorised persons.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: 6-hour deadline missed — RM500,000 fine plus RM5,000 per day continuing penalty. NC4 portal credentials untested and may fail during actual incident. False/misleading notification (s26(4)) carries RM300,000 fine — non-compoundable.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review tabletop exercise results with timeline. Test NC4 portal login for 2 authorised persons. Verify P.U.(A) 220/2024 form completeness.

controls.name: Incident Handling Procedures
controls.description: Define and execute incident handling procedures: triage, containment, eradication, and recovery per NCII system.
keyActivities: Define triage procedures with severity-based prioritisation
Implement containment strategies per incident type and NCII system
Execute eradication procedures to remove threat actors and artifacts
Manage recovery with verification before returning systems to production
controls.controlObjective: Effective incident handling limits damage through rapid triage, containment, eradication, and recovery. System-level playbooks ensure responders know exactly what to do for each NCII system type.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Triage procedures with severity-based prioritisation. Containment strategies defined per incident type and NCII system. Eradication procedures to remove threat actors and artifacts. Recovery verification before returning systems to production.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Incidents not contained — attacker persists and expands access. Eradication incomplete — threat actor regains access. Systems returned to production without verification — compromise continues.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review incident handling playbooks for 3 NCII system types. If incidents occurred, review handling records for 3 recent incidents.

controls.name: Digital Forensics Capability
controls.description: Maintain forensic capability including imaging tools, write-blockers, chain-of-custody forms, and qualified forensic personnel or third-party retainer.
keyActivities: Maintain forensic tool inventory (FTK Imager, dd, Guymager, write-blockers)
Document chain-of-custody procedures and forms
Maintain forensic workstation and investigation procedure
Establish third-party forensic retainer if outsourced (response SLA, certifications)
controls.controlObjective: Forensic capability preserves evidence for investigation, prosecution, and root cause analysis. Without proper tools and procedures, evidence is contaminated or lost — undermining both internal review and potential legal proceedings.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Forensic tool inventory: imaging tools (FTK Imager, dd), write-blockers, forensic workstation. Chain-of-custody forms and procedures documented. Investigation procedure aligned to ISO 27037. Third-party forensic retainer if capability outsourced (response SLA, certifications).
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Digital evidence contaminated or lost. Root cause analysis impossible. Prosecution of attackers undermined by poor evidence handling. Third-party forensic response delayed.
controls.samplingGuidance: Inspect forensic tool inventory. Review chain-of-custody procedure. If outsourced, verify retainer agreement and response SLA.

controls.name: Business Impact Analysis and RTO/RPO
controls.description: Conduct BIA identifying critical processes and dependent NCII systems. Define RTO and RPO per system and validate against actual recovery tests.
keyActivities: Conduct BIA with dependency mapping for NCII systems
Define RTO and RPO per critical process and NCII system
Obtain management approval of RTO/RPO targets
Validate RTO/RPO against actual recovery test results
controls.controlObjective: BIA identifies which NCII systems are most critical and how quickly they must be recovered. RTO/RPO targets drive recovery strategy design and backup architecture. Validation against actual test results ensures targets are achievable.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: BIA conducted with dependency mapping for all NCII systems. RTO and RPO defined per critical process and NCII system. Management approval of RTO/RPO targets. Actual recovery test results compared to RTO/RPO targets — gaps identified and addressed.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Recovery priorities unknown — all systems treated equally during crisis. RTO/RPO targets aspirational but never validated. Backup architecture misaligned to recovery requirements.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review BIA document. Verify RTO/RPO definitions for all NCII systems. Compare targets to actual recovery test results.

controls.name: Business Continuity Plan
controls.description: Maintain BCP with crisis management team, recovery strategies, communication plan, and offline documentation accessibility.
keyActivities: Establish Crisis Management Team with roles and activation criteria
Define recovery strategies per critical process (failover, backup, workarounds)
Develop communication plan (internal, external, media, regulators, NACSA)
Ensure BCP documentation accessible offline and at alternate sites
controls.controlObjective: The BCP provides the framework for maintaining essential services during and after a disruption. Crisis management team, recovery strategies, and communication plans ensure coordinated response.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: BCP document with crisis management team (CMT) established. Recovery strategies defined per critical process. Communication plan covering internal, external, media, regulators, and NACSA. BCP documentation accessible offline and at alternate sites.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: No coordinated response during crisis — ad-hoc decisions under pressure. Communication breakdown — stakeholders uninformed. BCP documentation inaccessible during disruption when it's most needed.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review BCP document. Verify CMT membership and contact information. Confirm offline accessibility of BCP.

controls.name: Backup and Recovery Operations
controls.description: Maintain backup operations with >99% completion rate. Conduct quarterly restoration tests with data validation. Ensure full NCII system coverage.
keyActivities: Monitor backup job completion rate (>99% target)
Investigate all backup failures and document resolution
Conduct quarterly restoration tests with data validation
Verify NCII system backup coverage (no gaps)
controls.controlObjective: Backup is the last line of defence against data loss. Completion rates, failure investigation, and quarterly restoration tests ensure backups will actually work when needed — not just appear to work.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Backup job completion rate > 99% with all failures investigated. All NCII systems covered by backup schedule (no gaps). Quarterly restoration tests with data validation confirming integrity. Backup media stored securely with appropriate retention.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Backup failures undetected until recovery is needed. Restoration test never conducted — backup integrity unknown. NCII system not in backup scope discovered only after data loss.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review 90 days of backup job logs — calculate completion rate. Verify NCII system backup coverage. Review last 2 restoration test records.

controls.name: BCP Testing and Simulation
controls.description: Conduct annual scenario-based BCP testing including tabletop exercises, live simulations, and DR failover tests. Document results and track action plans.
keyActivities: Conduct annual scenario-based testing (tabletop, live simulation, red team)
Execute DR failover test with actual failover timing and data integrity validation
Document post-test reports with gap identification
Track action plans with ownership, completion dates, and closure
controls.controlObjective: Plans that aren't tested are assumptions. Annual scenario-based testing validates the BCP under realistic conditions. DR failover tests prove recovery capability. Post-test reports drive continuous improvement.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Annual testing conducted using realistic scenarios (ransomware, data destruction, supply chain compromise). DR failover test executed — actual failover timing and data integrity validated. Post-test reports with identified gaps. Action plans with ownership, completion dates, and closure tracking.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: BCP contains untested assumptions. DR failover may fail during actual disaster. Known gaps from prior tests not addressed. Recovery time exceeds RTO under real conditions.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review last annual BCP test report. Verify DR failover test records with timing and data integrity validation. Review action plan closure status.

controls.name: Sector CoP Identification and Mapping
controls.description: Identify applicable sector-specific CoP and map sector requirements to entity operations. Engage with sector lead.
keyActivities: Identify and obtain applicable sector-specific CoP from sector lead
Map sector-specific requirements to entity operations and NCII systems
Engage with sector lead on interpretation and reporting obligations
Document sector CoP applicability assessment
controls.controlObjective: Each NCII sector has specific requirements defined by its sector lead. Identifying and mapping the applicable sector CoP ensures the entity meets obligations beyond the general CoP baseline.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Applicable sector-specific CoP identified and obtained from sector lead. Sector requirements mapped to entity operations and NCII systems. Ongoing engagement with sector lead on interpretation. Reporting obligations to sector lead met.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Entity complies with general CoP but misses sector-specific obligations. Sector lead engagement absent — entity isolated from sector-level threat sharing and guidance.
controls.samplingGuidance: Verify sector CoP identification. If CoP not yet published, document as scope limitation. Entity-level control.
