controls.name: OT/ICS/SCADA Security (if applicable)
controls.description: Implement OT-specific controls where applicable: OT/IT segmentation, OT vulnerability management, and OT-specific incident response.
keyActivities: Implement OT/IT network segmentation
Establish OT-specific vulnerability management and patching procedures
Develop OT-specific incident response playbooks
Conduct OT security risk assessment
controls.controlObjective: NCII entities in energy, transport, water, and other operational technology sectors must address OT-specific risks. IT security controls do not adequately protect ICS/SCADA environments — OT requires tailored segmentation, patching, and incident response.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: OT/IT network segmentation implemented with controlled cross-zone traffic. OT-specific vulnerability management and patching procedures (accounting for availability constraints). OT-specific incident response playbooks. OT security risk assessment conducted.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: IT compromise propagates to OT systems controlling physical infrastructure. OT systems unpatched due to availability concerns but without compensating controls. IT incident response procedures inappropriate for OT environments.
controls.samplingGuidance: N/A if entity has no OT systems. If applicable: verify OT/IT segmentation, review OT patching procedure, inspect OT-specific playbooks.

controls.name: Sector-Specific Regulatory Compliance
controls.description: Comply with sector-specific regulatory requirements beyond the CoP (e.g., BNM RMiT for banking, PCI-DSS for payment processing).
keyActivities: Identify sector-specific regulatory requirements (BNM RMiT, PCI-DSS, etc.)
Map dual-regulation requirements to avoid gaps and conflicts
Implement sector-specific controls where they exceed CoP baseline
Report compliance to both NACSA and sector regulator
controls.controlObjective: Many NCII entities operate under dual regulation — Act 854 plus sector-specific requirements (BNM RMiT for banking, MCMC for ICT, etc.). Mapping dual requirements prevents gaps where one regime requires more than the other.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Sector-specific regulatory requirements identified (e.g., BNM RMiT, PCI-DSS). Dual-regulation mapping showing where sector requirements exceed CoP baseline. Sector-specific controls implemented where they exceed CoP. Compliance reported to both NACSA and sector regulator.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Compliance with CoP only — sector-specific requirements not met. Regulatory action from sector regulator. Conflicting requirements between Act 854 and sector legislation unresolved.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review dual-regulation mapping. Verify 3 sector-specific controls that exceed CoP baseline. Conditional — only if sector regulation applies.

controls.name: Training Completion Monitoring
controls.description: Track training completion (target 100%) including role-specific training for NCII administrators, CSIRT members, and authorised persons.
keyActivities: Track training completion percentage by department (target: 100%)
Monitor role-specific training for NCII admins, CSIRT, authorised persons
Identify and remediate training gaps
Report training metrics to governance committee
controls.controlObjective: Training completion monitoring ensures awareness and competency requirements are actually met — not just planned. Role-specific tracking ensures NCII administrators and CSIRT members have the specialised training their roles demand.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Training completion tracked by department with target of 100%. Role-specific training records maintained for NCII administrators, CSIRT members, and authorised persons. Training gaps identified and remediated. Metrics reported to governance committee.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Training planned but not completed. Personnel operating NCII systems without required competency. CSIRT members untrained for incident response.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review training completion dashboard. Sample 10 personnel records — verify role-specific training completed.

controls.name: Incident Response Effectiveness Metrics
controls.description: Measure incident response effectiveness: MTTD, MTTR, mean time to contain, closure rate, and 6-hour notification compliance rate.
keyActivities: Measure MTTD, MTTR, and mean time to contain
Track incident closure rate and trend over time
Monitor 6-hour notification compliance rate per P.U.(A) 220/2024
Report metrics to governance committee with trend analysis
controls.controlObjective: Metrics transform incident response from anecdotal to evidence-based. MTTD, MTTR, and 6-hour notification compliance rate measure whether the entity can actually detect, respond, and report within required timeframes.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: MTTD, MTTR, and mean time to contain measured and reported. Incident closure rate tracked with trend analysis. 6-hour notification compliance rate per P.U.(A) 220/2024 calculated. Metrics presented to governance committee with trend analysis.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: No visibility into detection and response performance. 6-hour notification compliance unknown — entity may be consistently failing. No basis for improvement — cannot manage what you don't measure.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review metric dashboards. Verify raw incident data for 5 incidents to confirm MTTD/MTTR calculations.

controls.name: Compliance Monitoring and Reporting
controls.description: Produce security control implementation status reports and consolidated compliance monitoring reports (quarterly minimum) to governance committee.
keyActivities: Produce security control implementation status report across all CoP domains
Generate consolidated compliance monitoring report (quarterly minimum)
Present to governance committee with decisions and action items
Track action item closure
controls.controlObjective: Continuous compliance monitoring prevents the entity from discovering non-compliance only during biennial audit. Quarterly reporting to governance committee ensures leadership visibility and timely remediation.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Security control implementation status report covering all CoP domains. Consolidated compliance monitoring report produced quarterly (minimum) and presented to governance committee. Committee decisions and action items documented. Action item closure tracked.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Non-compliance discovered only during biennial audit — 2-year gap in visibility. Governance committee unaware of compliance posture. Action items from prior audits or assessments not tracked to closure.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review last 4 quarterly compliance reports. Verify governance committee minutes for discussion of compliance. Sample 10 action items — verify closure.

controls.name: Annual Compliance Review
controls.description: Conduct annual review of overall CoP compliance programme. Incorporate audit findings, incident lessons, and regulatory changes. Trigger ad-hoc reviews as needed.
keyActivities: Conduct annual review covering all CoP domains
Incorporate audit findings, incident lessons, and regulatory changes
Trigger reviews following significant incidents or regulatory changes
Document review findings with management approval
controls.controlObjective: Annual review provides a comprehensive reassessment of the compliance programme — incorporating audit findings, incident lessons, and regulatory changes. Triggered reviews ensure the programme adapts to significant events between annual cycles.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Annual review covering all CoP domains with documented outcomes. Audit findings, incident lessons learned, and regulatory changes incorporated. Triggered reviews conducted after significant incidents or regulatory changes. Management approval of review findings.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Compliance programme becomes stale. Lessons from incidents not incorporated. Regulatory changes missed until the next audit.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review last annual compliance review report. If triggered reviews occurred, verify documentation. Entity-level control.

controls.name: Compliance Gap Action Plan
controls.description: Maintain consolidated compliance gap action plan with owners, timelines, and closure tracking. Report to governance committee.
keyActivities: Maintain consolidated gap action plan with specific descriptions
Assign owners and realistic target completion dates
Track closure with evidence verification
Report trend analysis (new gaps vs closed gaps) to governance committee
controls.controlObjective: Identifying gaps without tracking remediation to closure creates a false sense of progress. The action plan with owners, timelines, and closure tracking ensures gaps are actively managed — not just documented.
controls.whatGoodLooksLike: Consolidated action plan with specific gap descriptions referencing CoP domain and requirement. Named owners with target completion dates. Current status tracked (Open/In Progress/Completed/Overdue). Closure evidence verified for completed items. Overdue items escalated. Trend analysis (new gaps vs closed gaps) reported.
controls.keyRiskIfAbsent: Gaps identified in audits, assessments, and reviews but not remediated. No accountability for remediation. Recurring findings cycle after cycle.
controls.samplingGuidance: Review action plan. Sample 10 items — verify 5 completed (closure evidence) and 5 open (realistic timelines). Check for overdue items.
